fact: August 10 is exactly 222 days into the year! That's like counting to 222 by ones - your mouth would get tired.
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak cricket season and male crickets are chirping their loudest to find girlfriends - you can actually count their chirps to figure out the temperature!
The sun rises almost 2 minutes later than it did just a week ago, and most people don't even notice summer is secretly sneaking away.
Why do fireflies seem extra magical around this time? August nights are getting just dark enough, just early enough, for peak firefly flashing contests.
Spider webs covered in morning dew become visible everywhere in late summer - August 10 sits right in the middle of 'spider web season' when orb weavers show off their best work.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, August 10 falls smack in the middle of winter, when wombats are at their fluffiest and furriest.
The zodiac sign Leo is roaring strong on August 10 - Leos are supposedly brave like lions, which is perfect since this date needs courage to face the end of summer vacation!
Tree leaves haven't started changing colors yet, but their green is getting deeper and richer as they soak up these final weeks of strong summer sunlight.
August 10 marks the time when monarch butterflies in North America start getting restless and begin their incredible 2,000-mile journey to Mexico.
In northern regions, wild berries like blackberries and blueberries are at their absolute juiciest and sweetest, making bears very happy and very fat.
Ocean water temperatures hit their warmest point of the year around August 10 in many coastal areas - the water has been soaking up summer heat for months!
